title: Microloma massonii
intro: Microloma massonii, also known as 'Makoni', is a perennial plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Apocynaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Microloma massonii include alkaloids, saponins, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Microloma massonii is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as tinctures and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Microloma massonii is classified in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family comprises around 5,000 species of flowering plants, including various genera such as Plumeria and Mandevilla. Microloma massonii is further classified in the subfamily Apocynoideae, a group of woody and herbaceous plants. The genus Microloma is part of the tribe Apocynaceae tribe Apocynoideae. This classification places Microloma massonii within the larger group of plants that are primarily characterized by their complex flowers and milky sap.
